generated_comment: It's encouraging to see more women in leadership roles, yes. But honestly, sometimes these high-profile promotions happen so quickly that you can't help but wonder if it's always pure merit, or if companies are also looking to tick diversity boxes. We need leaders who truly earn their way up the corporate ladder, not just get fast-tracked.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic as it questions the merit of a woman's promotion, implying she was 'fast-tracked' or appointed due to 'diversity boxes' rather than her competence. This undermines her achievements and speculates about her path to power, aligning with the 'merit undermining' and 'speculating about how a woman got a prestigious role' surface manifestations of F04.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
